title: zieria minutiflora subsp. trichocarpa
intro: Zieria minutiflora subsp. trichocarpa, also known as the small-flowered zieria, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to Australia. Traditionally, this plant has been used by Indigenous Australians for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat a range of health issues. The health benefits of Zieria minutiflora subsp. trichocarpa include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are often used to support overall health and well-being.
